articles.full_text:
100 km wind, thunderstorms, heavy rainfall and…: Delhi, Noida, UP, Bihar, Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh, Haryana, Punjab to…, check full IMD forecast here
Amid intense heatwave conditions in states like Uttar Pradesh, Bihar and Delhi, the IMD has issued a storm and heavy rain alert for 13 states. Eastern and northeastern India may witness thunderstorms and strong winds of up to 100 kmph in the next 16 hours. Meanwhile, heatwave conditions are expected to continue across North India for the next few days.
Amid intense heatwave conditions in states like Uttar Pradesh, Bihar and Delhi, the IMD has issued a storm and heavy rain alert for 13 states. Eastern and northeastern India may witness thunderstorms and strong winds of up to 100 kmph in the next 16 hours. Meanwhile, heatwave conditions are expected to continue across North India for the next few days.
Delhi and Uttar Pradesh to Face Severe Heatwave
Delhi-NCR is likely to witness severe heatwave conditions from May 21 to 26, with temperatures expected to touch 47 degree celsius. Strong hot winds may continue throughout the day. In Uttar Pradesh, cities like Lucknow, Varanasi, Kanpur and Prayagraj may face extreme heat, while Banda has already recorded 48.2 degree celsius.
Rain and Thunderstorm Alert in Bihar and Uttarakhand
The weather department has predicted rain and thunderstorms in Bihar between May 21 and 26. Cities like Patna, Bhagalpur and Muzaffarpur may witness strong winds and rainfall. In Uttarakhand, rain with thunder is expected on May 22 and 23 in areas including Dehradun, Nainital and Haridwar.
Rajasthan Under Red Alert for Heatwave
Rajasthan is expected to face severe heatwave conditions till May 26. Temperatures in eastern and western Rajasthan may reach up to 48 degree celsius. Cities like Jaipur, Jodhpur, Udaipur, Kota and Jaisalmer are likely to experience extreme heat.
Rain Likely in Himachal Pradesh and Jharkhand
Rain and strong winds may begin in Himachal Pradesh from May 21 to 23. Shimla, Manali and Dharamshala may witness cloudy skies and rainfall. Jharkhand may also experience thunderstorms and strong winds of up to 70 kmph in cities like Ranchi, Jamshedpur and Dhanbad.
Punjab to Continue Facing Intense Heat
Punjab is also likely to remain under severe heatwave conditions till May 26. Chandigarh may see temperatures around 45°C, while Amritsar, Ludhiana and Patiala could witness hot and dusty winds. The IMD has issued a red alert for heatwave conditions in the state.
